audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Deuteronomy / Devarim not-by-righteousness, golden-calf, and Moses-intercession discourse, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for conquest warning against self-righteousness, Anakim, dispossession rhetoric, Horeb fast, calf apostasy, broken tablets, Aaron intercession, Taberah/Massah/Kibroth memory, Kadesh refusal, and forty-day intercession.

verses.restored: Hear, Israel: You are crossing Jordan today to enter and possess nations greater and mightier than you, cities great and fortified to heavens,
a people great and tall, sons of Anakim, whom you know and of whom you heard, Who can stand before sons of Anak?

Know today that YHWH your Divine is crossing before you as consuming fire; he will destroy them and humble them before you, and you shall dispossess and cause them to perish quickly, as YHWH spoke to you.

Do not say in your heart, when YHWH your Divine thrusts them out from before you, Because of my righteousness YHWH brought me in to possess this land; rather, because of wickedness of these nations YHWH dispossesses them from before you.

Not because of your righteousness or uprightness of your heart are you entering to possess their land, but because of wickedness of these nations YHWH your Divine dispossesses them and to establish word YHWH swore to your fathers, Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
Know that not because of your righteousness YHWH your Divine gives you this good land to possess, for a stiff-necked people are you.
Remember, do not forget, how you provoked YHWH your Divine in wilderness; from day you came out from land of Egypt until you came to this place, you have been rebellious with YHWH.
At Horeb you provoked YHWH, and YHWH was angry with you to destroy you.
When I went up mountain to receive tablets of stone, tablets of covenant YHWH cut with you, I remained on mountain forty days and forty nights; bread I did not eat and water I did not drink.
YHWH gave me two tablets of stone written with finger of Divine, and upon them according to all words YHWH spoke with you at mountain from midst of fire on day of assembly.
At end of forty days and forty nights YHWH gave me two tablets of stone, tablets of covenant.

YHWH said to me, Rise, go down quickly from here, for your people whom you brought out from Egypt have acted corruptly; they turned quickly from way I commanded them and made for themselves molten image.
YHWH spoke to me, saying, I have seen this people, and look, a stiff-necked people it is.
Let me alone, and I will destroy them and blot out their name from under heavens, and I will make you a nation mightier and more numerous than they.
I turned and went down from mountain while mountain burned with fire, and two tablets of covenant were in my two hands.
I saw, and look, you had sinned/misaligned against YHWH your Divine; you made for yourselves a molten calf. You turned quickly from way YHWH commanded you.
I seized two tablets and threw them from my two hands and broke them before your eyes.
I fell before YHWH as at first, forty days and forty nights; bread I did not eat and water I did not drink, because of all your sin/misalignment you sinned, doing evil in eyes of YHWH to provoke him.
For I feared anger and fury with which YHWH was angry against you to destroy you. But YHWH listened to me also that time.
Against Aaron YHWH was very angry to destroy him, and I prayed also for Aaron at that time.
Your sin/misalignment that you made, calf, I took and burned with fire, crushed it, grinding thoroughly until fine as dust, and threw its dust into brook descending from mountain.
At Taberah, at Massah, and at Kibroth-hattaavah you provoked YHWH.
When YHWH sent you from Kadesh-barnea, saying, Go up and possess land I gave you, you rebelled against mouth/command of YHWH your Divine and did not trust him or listen to his voice.
Rebellious you have been with YHWH from day I knew you.
I fell before YHWH forty days and forty nights that I fell, because YHWH said he would destroy you.
I prayed to YHWH and said, Lord YHWH, do not destroy your people and your inheritance whom you redeemed in your greatness, whom you brought out from Egypt with strong hand.
Remember your servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob. Do not turn toward stubbornness of this people, or its wickedness, or its sin/misalignment,
lest land from which you brought us say, Because YHWH was not able to bring them into land he spoke to them and because he hated them, he brought them out to put them to death in wilderness.
Yet they are your people and your inheritance whom you brought out by your great power and outstretched arm.

translation_notes: The chapter's central claim is anti-triumphal: possession is not proof of Israel's superior righteousness.
Calling conquered nations wicked does not morally solve the violence of dispossession or permit later empires to label targeted peoples evil.
Stiff-necked language is internal covenant critique, not permission for antisemitic stereotypes about Jewish stubbornness or rejection.
Moses refuses the offer to replace Israel with a nation descended from himself and intercedes for the threatened people.
His fasting is narrative intercession, not a mandate for medically dangerous fasting or self-punishment.
The broken tablets embody covenant rupture, while Moses' repeated prayer preserves the possibility of renewal.
safety_note: Do not use Deuteronomy 9 to justify conquest by declaring another people wicked, to promote antisemitic 'stiff-necked' tropes, or to claim success proves righteousness. Center humility, refusal of replacement theology, leadership that intercedes rather than profits from destruction, and covenant renewal after failure.
